title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After purchasing the alarm clocks, Bill Everly, Jimmy Sycamore, and the others gather in Gerry Wade’s bedroom to hide them. They debate whether the alarms should ring simultaneously or sequentially, ultimately deciding to set them to go off one after another, beginning at 6:30 am. When Rupert Bettman suggests that Gerry might hear the ticking of the clocks once he is in bed, the group—referring to Bettman as the "chimpanzee" due to his intelligence—decides to wait until Gerry is asleep before sneaking in to place the clocks on the floor.

Meanwhile, a bridge game is underway. Osborne-Smith and Bettman play with high concentration, while Gerry Wade and Mrs. Cutt play a more casual game. Sir Oswald and Lady Cutt are paired together; Sir Oswald frequently criticizes Lady Cutt's play, though she remains gracious. During the game, Lady Cutt wins a trick and collects ten shillings from Sir Oswald.

At 12:30 am, the guests begin to depart. Ronnie DeVille, who lives next door to Gerry, acts as a lookout. At 1:45 am, Ronnie informs the group that Gerry’s light has gone out and he appears to be sleeping soundly. The conspirators gather in the hallway to finalize their plan. To avoid making noise, they decide to pass the clocks into the room one by one rather than entering as a group. After dismissing several candidates because of their size or tendency to giggle, the group selects Bettman as the most suitable person to enter the room.
